*** 1 FATAL *** THE GILLESPIE COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE AND FREDERICKSBURG FIRE EMS RESPONDED TO A REPORT OF A VEHICLE THAT HAD BEEN SWEPT AWAY BY FAST-MOVING WATER AT THE PEDERNALES RIVER CROSSING ON ALFRED PETSCH ROAD. A FAMILY OF FOUR PEOPLE WERE IN THE VEHICLE. THREE MEMBERS ESCAPED UNHARMED BUT A 14-YEAR-OLD FEMALE WAS MISSING AND LATER PRONOUNCED DECEASED AS SHE WAS FOUND ONE MILE DOWNRIVER.

A SUSPECTED MICROBURST CAUSED STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE IN THE CASTELL AREA DURING THE EARLY MORNING HOURS OF APRIL 12. TREES WERE DAMAGED ACROSS THE AREA, WITH A CHIMNEY BEING KNOCKED DOWN OFF A HOUSE ON FM 2768. THE GARAGE DOOR WAS BLOWN OUT ON THE VOLUNTEER FIRE DEPARTMENT BUILDING OFF OF CR 152, AND AN RV WAS TIPPED OVER EAST OF CASTELL ALSO OFF CR 152. AT THIS SAME TRAILER LOCATION ABOUT 2 MILES EAST OF CASTELL, SECTIONS OF A METAL ROOF WERE ALSO TAKEN OFF A HOUSE.
THERE ARE REPORTS OF POWER LINES DOWN NEAR CASTELL AND A ROOF RIPPED OFF A HOUSE A FEW MILES EAST OF CASTELL ON RANCH ROAD 152.

PICTURES RECEIVED OF SEVERAL TREES AND BRANCHES NEAR 1 FOOT IN DIAMETER ALONG COUNTY ROAD 1631. BALES OF HAY ALSO THROWN ACROSS HERBER-SHAFER ROAD AND SMALL BRANCHES AROUND 1 TO 2 INCHES IN DIAMETER DOWN ON WAHRMUND-AHRENS ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR. DAMAGE MAY BE TORNADIC BUT UNCONFIRMED AT THIS TIME.

DAMAGE HAS BEEN REPORTED AT HOFBRAU RV PARK JUST NORTH OF ROUND MOUNTAIN ALONG US HIGHWAY 281 COINCIDENT WITH WHERE A STRONG ROTATIONAL SIGNATURE ON RADAR TRAVERSED. DAMAGE INCLUDES TREES DOWNED/UPROOTED AND SOME DAMAGE TO RVS. A DUAL POL TORNADO DEBRIS SIGNATURE (TDS) WAS OBSERVED SHORTLY AFTER THE CIRCULATION PASSED OVER THIS LOCATION. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

DELAYED REPORT FROM SUNDAY MAY 24....A NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT A WIDE SWATH OF SEVERE 60 TO 70 MPH WINDS CAUSED EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE TO AREAS NEAR CARTWRIGHT TRAIL, TALLEY ROAD, AS WELL AS AREAS ALONG CULEBRA ROAD. FENCES WERE BLOWN DOWN, SHEDS OVERTURNED, AND SOME ROOF DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED TO HOMES.
